title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A disability-led local news cooperative. They need fingerprinted licensed media, clean-equivalent incremental builds, accessible articles and navigation, responsive reflow, progressive enhancement, representative performance budgets, and human review evidence across mobile, tablet, desktop, zoom, long content, and disabled JavaScript. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Responsive Incremental Publication with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension.…
objectives: Hash canonical bytes with recorded algorithm and use the digest for cache-busting identity without embedding unstable metadata.
Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.
Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Content Fingerprint
steps.instruction:
project case — A project publishing team handles case 1395 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Hash canonical bytes with recorded algorithm and use the digest for cache-busting identity without embedding unstable metadata.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Content Fingerprint.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for hash canonical bytes with recorded algorithm and use the digest for cache-busting identity without embedding unstable metadata. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-rer9r9" that uses case 1380 to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Performance Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Performance Evidence, keep Performance Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-rer9r9
# Competency: ssg-performance-evidence.
# Case 1380: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_performance_evidence_rer9r9(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rer9r9 = "rer9r9-9r9rer-1380"
    assert evidence_variant_rer9r9
    evidence_axes_rer9r9 = (
        "readerrer9r9",
        "source1380",
        "parse9r9rer",
        "routerer9r91380",
        "artifact1380rer9r9",
        "recovery9r9rer1380",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rer9r9)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"
